item.description:

开始一个新项目

  • 从某人那里获得一些有用的建议......或其他任何可能引发快乐的事情❤️

    Have a wonderful weekend

item.title: O problema da "escrita-dupla" | O problema da "escrita-dupla"
item.description:
问题

有问题吗？ 查看适用于 enviada 的 nossa mensagem não，o DynamoDB não tem como fazer rollback 😱。

乌玛历史真实

科莫评论 não foi enviada para ofluxo de aprovação, ela ficará pendente para SEMPRE!。

乌玛解决方案“ingênua”

观察 no diagrama acima, quando tentamos fazer o rollback chamando repository.delete o DynamoDB pod nos retornar um erro 400. Logo, nesse cenário a nossa review continuará pendente 。

结论

Recapitulando o que aprendemos até aqui: Recapitulando o que aprendemos até aqui:

  1. Cuidado ao invocar sistemas distribuídos em sequência sem contexto transacional. Cuidado ao invocar sistemas distribuídos em sequência sem contexto transacional。 Isso é receita para problemas de escrita-dupla e inconsistência dos dados. Isso é receita para problemas de escrita-dupla e inconsistência dos dados。
  2. Algumas vezes uma solução simples pode ser tudo que você precisa dependendo da sua escala. Algumas vezes uma solução simples pode ser tudo que você precisa dependendo da sua escala。
  3. Embora não seja o foco aqui, você agora sabe que existem outros padrões para lidar com esse tipo de erro. Embora não seja o foco aqui, você agora sabe que existem outros padrões para lidar com esse tipo de erro。

Eu espero que você tenha curtido. Eu espero que você tenha curtido。 Se gostou, não deixe de acompanhar as minhas dicas no twitter @hugaomarques . Se gostou, não deixe de acompanhar as minhas dicas no twitter @hugaomarques 。

Agradecimentos especiais ao @rponte e ao @zanfranceschi por revisarem o artigo e colaborarem com idéias. Agradecimentos especiais ao @rponte e ao @zanfranceschi por revisarem o artigo e colaborarem com idéias。



原文:

Aviso: Esse artigo é baseado em fatos reais 😬.

Nesse artigo eu vou te apresentar o problema da escrita dupla ("dual write") e as consequência desse problema em um sistema real que eu trabalhei. Eu não espero te apresentar uma resposta definitiva sobre como lidar com o problema mas eu espero que essa introdução faça você entender o problema, pensar sobre ele, saber identificá-lo e evitá-lo.

Problema

Vamos começar descrevendo o nosso problema. Nós tínhamos um sistema que fazia o seguinte:

  1. Usuário cria uma nova review
  2. O reviewService cria a review no repositório como pendente.
  3. O reviewService invoca o approvalService enviando uma mensagem para que o sistema de moderação aprove ou não a review. Esse processamente ocorre assíncronamente.
  4. O sistema responde a requisição com o ID da review pendente para que o usuário possa verificar o status depois, se ela foi aprovada ou rejeitada.

Em código o nosso caso de uso ficaria assim:


package com.hugomarques; public class ReviewController { private final ReviewRepository reviewsRepository; private final ApprovalWorkflow approvalService; ReviewController(ReviewRepository repository) { this.repository = repository; } @PostMapping("/reviews") Review newReview(Review newReview) { var pendingReview = reviewsRepository.save(newReview); approvalService.send(pendingReview); return pendingReview; }
}

Anos atrás, em meados de 2009-2010, esse código provavelmente seria implantado em um servidor JBoss, o repository seria um MySQL ou PostgresSQL e o approvalService estaria por detrás de uma fila JMS. E por que esses detalhes são importantes? Essas tecnologias em conjunto garantiam o controle transacional, ou seja, se enviar a mensagem para fila falha, a transação seria desfeita no banco de dados.

Hoje em dia, esse mesmo sistema (por vários motivos) poderia ser implementado sem o JBoss como servidor de aplicação, o repository poderia usar o AWS DynamoDB e o messageria poderia utilizar o AWS SQS. Qual o problema que isso nos trás? Nós não temos mais o controle transacional. Se a nossa mensagem não for enviada, o DynamoDB não tem como fazer rollback 😱.

Uma história real

Um dos times que eu trabalhei se deparou com esse problema um tempo atrás. Um belo dia a equipe começou a receber tickets que algumas reviews estavam ficando bloqueadas em status pendente.

Eu era o engenheiro on-call e decidi fazer uma análise. Eu verifiquei que toda vez que a review estava pendente o nosso código emitia uma exception de "Falha ao enviar mensagem para a fila".

Eu fui analisar o código e vi algo parecido com o exemplo desse artigo. Pra fazer as coisas ainda piores o código que enviava a mensagem estava contido dentro de um bloco try/catch, o catch logava o erro e não fazia mais nada. Ou seja, a operação era retornada com sucesso para o usuário!

Se você não entendeu o problema, em vez do fluxo ideal que temos no exemplo esse fluxo de erro era o seguinte:

  1. Usuário cria uma nova review
  2. O reviewService cria a review no repositório como pendente.
  3. O reviewService invoca o approvalService enviando uma mensagem para que o sistema de moderação aprove ou não a review. Esse processamente ocorre assíncronamente.
  4. A mensagem por qualquer motivo que seja falha ao ser enviada.
  5. O reviewService loga uma mensagem "Falha ao enviar mensagem para a fila".
  6. O reviewService retorna a review pendente ao usuário.
  7. Como a review não foi enviada para o fluxo de aprovação, ela ficará pendente para SEMPRE!.

Uma solução "ingênua"

Depois que achamos o problema, nós decidimos aplicar a solução mais simples possível: Tratar o erro diretamente no código, capturando o erro/exception e executando uma chamada de rollback para o serviço 1. O nosso novo fluxo fica da seguinte forma:

Vamos ver a implementação:


package com.hugomarques; public class ReviewController { private final ReviewRepository repository; private final ApprovalWorkflow approvalWorkflowService; ReviewController(ReviewRepository repository) { this.repository = repository; } @PostMapping("/reviews") Review newReview(Review newReview) { var pendingReview = repository.save(newReview); try { var approvedReview = approvalWorkflowService.start(pendingReview); return approvedReview; catch (Exception e) { repository.delete(pendingReview); } }
}

Notem que eu chamei a solução de ingênua. Por quê? O que acontece se o seu serviço tiver um problema de erro justo no rollback?

Observe no diagrama acima, quando tentamos fazer o rollback chamando repository.delete o DynamoDB pode nos retornar um erro 400. Logo, nesse cenário a nossa review continuará pendente.

Embora ingênua, a solução reduziu o número de inconsistências em mais de 90%. Por ser uma solução barata e simples, ela foi suficiente para "estancar o sangramento".

Para uma solução mais robusta o time teria que rearquitetar algumas partes do sistema e usar alguns padrões de microserviços como SAGAs e/ou "Transactional Outbox" mas isso são cenas para os próximos capítulos.

Conclusão

Recapitulando o que aprendemos até aqui:

  1. Cuidado ao invocar sistemas distribuídos em sequência sem contexto transacional. Isso é receita para problemas de escrita-dupla e inconsistência dos dados.
  2. Algumas vezes uma solução simples pode ser tudo que você precisa dependendo da sua escala.
  3. Embora não seja o foco aqui, você agora sabe que existem outros padrões para lidar com esse tipo de erro.

Eu espero que você tenha curtido. Se gostou, não deixe de acompanhar as minhas dicas no twitter @hugaomarques.

Agradecimentos especiais ao @rponte e ao @zanfranceschi por revisarem o artigo e colaborarem com idéias.
item.author: hugaomarques

item.title: 如何开发 Linux 应用程序（第 2 部分） | How to Develop Linux Applications (Part 2)
item.description:
蒂娜黄

发表于应用图像

  • 平板包装
  • 快手
  • 有用的网址
    • 新的应用程序会带有一个大的品牌横幅。 此外，热门和最近更新的应用程序会出现在首页上。
    • 定义应用的关键字、类别和子类别以帮助人们找到它。
    • 借助内置的社交媒体共享和应用 URL，用户可以直接从 AppCenter 轻松共享您的应用。

    您需要确保在src文件夹中为Application.vala文件添加前缀，并且它与您的代码许可证相匹配并为您分配版权。

     /* * SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2021 Your Name <you@email.com> */

    您的 MetaInfo 文件将包含在 AppCenter 中列出您的应用所需的所有信息，并且看起来类似于：

     <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <component type="desktop-application"> <id>com.github.myteam.myapp</id> <name>My App</name> <summary>Proves that we can use Vala and Gtk</summary> <metadata_license>CC-BY-4.0</metadata_license> <project_license>GPL-3.0-or-later</project_license> <description> <p> A quick summary of your app's main selling points and features. Just a couple sentences per paragraph is best </p> </description> <launchable type="desktop-id">com.github.myteam.myapp.desktop</launchable> </component>

    您的桌面条目文件将包含在应用程序菜单和 Dock 中显示您的应用程序所需的所有信息，并且应该如下所示：

     [Desktop Entry] Version=1.0 Type=Application Name=My App Comment=Proves that we can use Vala and Gtk Categories=Development;Education; Icon=com.github.myteam.myapp Exec=com.github.myteam.myapp Terminal=false

    在此处查看我们的第一个应用程序以获取其余代码以及完整的说明和详细信息。

    返回目录

    应用图像

    以 AppImage 格式分发您的桌面 Linux 应用程序，并赢得运行所有常见 Linux 发行版的用户。

    它使用系统包管理器来解决应用程序依赖关系并创建一个完整的包。 它可用于打包几乎任何类型的应用程序，包括使用 C/C++、Python 和 Java 制作的应用程序。

    有教程和故障排除文档以获取更多信息。

    如何安装 appimage-builder

    选项 1：适用于 amd64 系统的 AppImage

     wget -O appimage-builder-x86_64.AppImage https://github.com/AppImageCrafters/appimage-builder/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.1/appimage-builder-1.0.0-677acbd-x86_64.AppImage chmod +x appimage-builder-x86_64.AppImage # install (optional) sudo mv appimage-builder-x86_64.AppImage /usr/local/bin/appimage-builder

    选项 2：Docker 映像

    注意：此格式不支持测试 AppImages。 始终使用--skip-test。

     docker pull appimagecrafters/appimage-builder:latest

    选项 3：手动安装

    该项目使用 Python 3 构建，并使用各种命令行应用程序来实现其目标。 根据主机系统和配方，提供此类应用程序的软件包可能会有所不同。

    第 1 步：安装依赖项
    第 2 步：安装 appimage-builder 选项 1：安装最新的标记版本： 

     sudo pip3 install appimage-builder

    选项 2：安装开发版本：

     sudo pip3 install git+https://github.com/AppImageCrafters/appimage-builder.git

    第三步：安装appimagetool

    因此，我们必须使用以下解决方法来使 appimagetool 正常工作。

     # Install appimagetool AppImage sudo wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage -O /opt/appimagetool # workaround AppImage issues with Docker cd /opt/; sudo chmod +x appimagetool; sed -i 's|AI\x02|\x00\x00\x00|' appimagetool; sudo ./appimagetool --appimage-extract sudo mv /opt/squashfs-root /opt/appimagetool.AppDir sudo ln -s /opt/appimagetool.AppDir/AppRun /usr/local/bin/appimagetool

    返回目录

    平板包装

    为每个发行版构建

  • 开发与新版本 Linux 发行版兼容的应用程序
  • 使用Flathub使您的应用程序可用
  • 查看他们的开发人员指南或查看可以使用 Flatpak 的发行版数量。

    阅读Flatpak 文档，了解使用 Flatpak 构建和分发应用程序所需的一切。

    构建你的第一个 Flatpak是一个很好的起点！ 他们甚至有技巧和窍门

    快手

    构建快照并将其发布到Snap 商店

    此文件描述了 snap 的构建依赖项和运行时要求，它集成了远程存储库和扩展，并运行自定义脚本和挂钩以更好地与 CI 系统集成。

    顶级元数据包含商店通常使用的值

    • - architectures - assumes - base - compression - contact - description - hooks - issues - icon - layout - license - summary - title - type - version - plugs
    1. 应用程序和服务描述了应用程序和服务如何暴露给主机系统
    - adapter - autostart - command - command-chain - desktop - environment - install-mode - listen-stream - restart-condition - refresh-mode
    1. 部分描述了如何导入和构建快照的每个必需部分
    - build-attributes - build-snaps - organize - parse-info - source - stage

    有关更多信息，请查看文档指南、安装 snapd 、发布您的应用程序、管理 snap和故障排除。

    返回目录

    有用的网址

    你会成为一名 Linux 应用程序开发人员吗？

    快乐编码！

    亚历克斯 M. Schapelle - Apr 23 4月23日



    原文:

    Tina Huynh

    Posted on May 7

    1. AppCenter Dashboard
    2. AppImage
    3. Flatpak
    4. Snapcraft
    5. Helpful Links
    • New apps get featured with a large, branded banner. Plus, trending and recently-updated apps are featured on the front page.
    • Define your app's keywords, categories, and subcategories to help people find it.
    • With built-in social media sharing and app URLs, users can easily share your app right from AppCenter.

    You need to make sure you prefix your Application.vala file in your src folder and that it matches your license of your code and assigns copyright to you.

    /*
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later
 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2021 Your Name <you@email.com>
 */

    Your MetaInfo file will contain all the information needed to list your app in AppCenter and will look something like:

    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<component type="desktop-application">
  <id>com.github.myteam.myapp</id>

  <name>My App</name>
  <summary>Proves that we can use Vala and Gtk</summary>

  <metadata_license>CC-BY-4.0</metadata_license>
  <project_license>GPL-3.0-or-later</project_license>

  <description>
    <p>
      A quick summary of your app's main selling points and features. Just a couple sentences per paragraph is best
    </p>
  </description>

  <launchable type="desktop-id">com.github.myteam.myapp.desktop</launchable>
</component>

    Your desktop entry file will contain all the information needed to display your app in the applications menu as well as the dock and should look something like this:

    [Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Type=Application

Name=My App
Comment=Proves that we can use Vala and Gtk
Categories=Development;Education;

Icon=com.github.myteam.myapp
Exec=com.github.myteam.myapp
Terminal=false

    Check Our First App here for the rest of the code and full explanation and details.

    Back to TOC

    AppImage

    Distribute your desktop Linux application in the AppImage format and win users running all common Linux distributions.

    appimage-builder is a novel tool for creating AppImages. It uses the system package manager to resolve the application dependencies and creates a complete bundle. It can be used to pack almost any kind of applications including those made using: C/C++, Python, and Java.

    There are tutorials as well as troubleshooting documentation for more information.

    How to Install appimage-builder

    Option 1: AppImage for amd64 systems

    wget -O appimage-builder-x86_64.AppImage https://github.com/AppImageCrafters/appimage-builder/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.1/appimage-builder-1.0.0-677acbd-x86_64.AppImage
chmod +x appimage-builder-x86_64.AppImage

# install (optional)
sudo mv appimage-builder-x86_64.AppImage /usr/local/bin/appimage-builder

    Option 2: Docker Image

    NOTE: Testing AppImages is not supported on this format. Always use –skip-test.

    docker pull appimagecrafters/appimage-builder:latest

    Option 3: Manual Installation

    The project is built using Python 3 and uses various command-line applications to fulfill its goal. Depending on the host system and the recipe the packages providing such applications may vary.

    Step 1: Installing dependencies
    Debian/Ubuntu

    sudo apt install -y python3-pip python3-setuptools patchelf desktop-file-utils libgdk-pixbuf2.0-dev fakeroot strace fuse

# Install appimagetool AppImage
sudo wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage -O /usr/local/bin/appimagetool
sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/appimagetool

    Arch Linux

    sudo pacman -Sy python-pip python-setuptools binutils patchelf desktop-file-utils gdk-pixbuf2 wget fakeroot strace

# Install appimagetool AppImage
sudo wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage -O /usr/local/bin/appimagetool
sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/appimagetool

    Step 2: Installing appimage-builder Option 1: Installing latest tagged release:

    sudo pip3 install appimage-builder

    Option 2: Installing development version:

    sudo pip3 install git+https://github.com/AppImageCrafters/appimage-builder.git

    Step 3: Install appimagetool

    There is an issue in the AppImage runtime format that prevents it proper execution inside docker containers. Therefore we must use the following workaround to make appimagetool work properly.

    # Install appimagetool AppImage
sudo wget https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit/releases/download/continuous/appimagetool-x86_64.AppImage -O /opt/appimagetool

# workaround AppImage issues with Docker
cd /opt/; sudo chmod +x appimagetool; sed -i 's|AI\x02|\x00\x00\x00|' appimagetool; sudo ./appimagetool --appimage-extract
sudo mv /opt/squashfs-root /opt/appimagetool.AppDir
sudo ln -s /opt/appimagetool.AppDir/AppRun /usr/local/bin/appimagetool

    Back to TOC

    Flatpak

    Flatpak is a framework for distributing desktop applications on Linux and runs as an independent open source project.

    • Build for every distro
    • Develop apps compatible with new versions of Linux distributions
    • Make your apps available with Flathub
    • Flatpak is developed by an independent community

    Check their developer guide or see the number of distros Flatpak can be used with.

    Read the Flatpak documentation to get everything you need to know to build and distribute applications using Flatpak.

    Building your first Flatpak is a great place to start! They even have tips and tricks

    Snapcraft

    Build and publish your snaps to the Snap store

    At the heart of the snapcraft build process is a file called snapcraft.yaml. This file describes a snap’s build dependencies and run-time requirements, it integrates remote repositories and extensions, and runs custom scripts and hooks for better integration with CI systems.

    snapcraft.yaml is organized into three principle sections: top-level metadata, apps and services, and parts.

    1. top-level metadata contains values typically used by the store
    - architectures
- assumes
- base
- compression
- contact
- description
- hooks
- issues
- icon
- layout
- license
- summary
- title
- type
- version
- plugs
    1. app and services describes how the app and services are exposed to the host system
    - adapter
- autostart
- command
- command-chain
- desktop
- environment
- install-mode
- listen-stream
- restart-condition
- refresh-mode
    1. parts describes how to import and build each required part of the snap
    - build-attributes
- build-snaps
- organize
- parse-info
- source
- stage

    For more information, check documentation guidelines, installing snapd, releasing your app, managing snaps, and troubleshooting.

    Back to TOC

    Helpful Links

    Will you become a Linux app developer?

    Happy coding!

    Alex M. Schapelle - Apr 23
item.author: tmchuynh

item.title: Place iOS - r/place 画布克隆 | Place iOS - r/place canvas clone
item.description:
耶稣·格拉

发表于我决定为 Appwrite Hackathon 创建这个项目，以便在 SwiftUI 中测试我并使用大多数 appwrite API。 我是像素艺术的超级粉丝，这个项目非常适合它。 规则很简单：您可以每 30 秒放置一个像素，并且画布将使用realtime更新。
使用 appwrite 管理帐户、数据库、存储、会话、实时、功能等都非常简单。 为了添加更多有趣的东西，我在 python 中制作了一个机器人，用于在画布中创建像素艺术。🤖

提交类别：

移动大亨

链接到代码

我决定为 Appwrite Hackathon 创建这个项目，以便在 SwiftUI 中测试我并使用大多数 appwrite Api'。




其他资源/信息

快速浏览

视频

完整的视频演示

画布视图和可用颜色

有趣的事实：当我做像素艺术时，调色板是我最喜欢的调色板。 每 30 秒放置一个像素。

我做了一个功能来管理这个。

登陆注册。

只有成员才能放置像素。

机器人🤖。

另外为了好玩，我用python做了一个机器人。
该机器人将颜色更改为允许的颜色，并且看起来非常好。 结论

这个项目做起来超级有趣。
感谢您阅读并特别感谢 Appwrite 社区解决了我的问题。 马特乌斯·罗德里格斯 - 康斯坦丁·塞米亚诺夫—— Jan 12 '21 21 年 1 月 12 日



原文:

Jesus Guerra

Posted on May 7

Place iOS is r/place canvas clone made with Appwrite and pure SwiftUI. I decide create this project for Appwrite Hackathon to test me in SwiftUI and use majority appwrite Apis. 😃

I'm a super fan of pixel art and this project fit perfect whit it. 🟩🟨🟪


The rules are simple: You can place a pixel every 30 seconds and the canvas will be updated using realtime.
Manage accounts, database, storage, sessions, realtime, functions and more were super simple with appwrite. 😎

For add more fun stuff, i made a bot in python for create pixel art in canvas.🤖

Mobile Moguls

I decide create this project for Appwrite Hackathon to test me in SwiftUI and use majority appwrite Api'.




Additional Resources / Info

Video

Fun fact: The palette is my favorite palette when i did pixel art. PICO-8

Place a pixel every 30 seconds.

I made a function to manage this.

Log in/Sign up.

Only members are allowed to place pixels.

Bot 🤖.

Additionally for fun i made a bot in python.
The bot change the colors for the allowed colors and looks pretty fine. 😎

Conclusion

This project was super fun to do.

item.title: NestJS ⚡ 使用 TypeORM 的多个数据库设置 | NestJS ⚡ Multiple DB Setup with TypeORM
item.description:
嗨，在这篇文章中，我将通过一个简单的示例来描述如何设置和使用多个数据库连接。

我将假设您已经创建了一个 NestJS 应用程序并准备好连接到您的 2 个或更多数据库。 让我们直奔主题。

让我们开始吧

定义数据库连接选项

在 NestJS 文档之后，我们将找到直接在代码上设置的连接选项示例凭据。

 ... @ Module ({ imports : [ TypeOrmModule . forRoot ({ type : ' mysql ' , host : ' localhost ' , port : 3306 , username : ' root ' , password : ' root ' , database : ' test ' , entities : [ Customer ], synchronize : true , }), ], }) ...

我们应该使用环境配置文件或类似的策略，而不是像这样在代码上公开凭据。

对于本示例，我们将使用@nestjs/config包从环境文件中获取数据库凭据。

 ... @ Module ({ imports : [ ConfigModule . forRoot ({ isGlobal : true , }), TypeOrmModule . forRootAsync ({ imports : [ ConfigModule ], inject : [ ConfigService ], useFactory : async ( configService : ConfigService ) => { return { type : ' mssql ' , host : configService . get ( ' MAIN_DB_HOST ' ), port : parseInt ( configService . get ( ' MAIN_DB_PORT ' )), database : configService . get ( ' MAIN_DB_DATABASE ' ), username : configService . get ( ' MAIN_DB_USERNAME ' ), password : configService . get ( ' MAIN_DB_PASSWORD ' ), schema : configService . get ( ' MAIN_DB_SCHEMA ' ), entities : [ Customer ], synchronize : false , }; }, }), ...

我们不需要为我们的第一个连接定义连接名称。 默认情况下，它将被命名为“默认”😬。 对于任何其他已定义的连接，我们需要提供一个名称。

现在，因为我们从.env文件中获取凭据并使用forRootAsync ，所以我们需要在useFactory函数👀之外添加连接name属性。 这是一个非常重要的细节，文档中的任何地方都没有提到。

最终的app.module.ts文件应如下所示。

实体

为了这个练习，我们创建了两个实体。 客户实体将使用主数据库连接，而 AccessLog 实体将使用辅助数据库连接。 有关 TypeOrm 实体和存储库模式的更多信息，请查看NestJS TypeOrm Docs和TypeOrm Docs 。

使用主数据库连接

为了能够访问客户实体 TypeOrm 功能，我们需要将 TypeOrm 导入客户模块。

这就是我们在客户服务中使用它的方式。

使用辅助数据库连接

正如我们对主连接和客户实体所做的那样，我们需要将 TypeOrm 导入 AccessLog 模块，但这次我们还需要将连接名称传递给TypeOrmModule.forFeature([Entities, ...], connectionName)方法.

这就是我们在 AccessLog 服务中使用它的方式。

结论

✅ We managed to setup a multiple db configuration with credentials values coming from an environment file. ✅ 我们设法使用来自环境文件的凭据值设置了多个数据库配置。 ✅ Defined what connection should each entity use. ✅ 定义了每个实体应该使用什么连接。 ✅ With the knowledge of how to add a secondary connection we should be able to add many more as needed. ✅ 了解如何添加辅助连接后，我们应该能够根据需要添加更多连接。

✅ Learned about the little gotchas when initializing TypeOrm asynchronously. ✅ 了解异步初始化 TypeOrm 时的小问题。



原文:

Hi, in this post I will describe how to setup and use multiple database connections with a simple example.

I will proceed under the assumption that you already have created a NestJS application and have your 2 or more databases setup ready to be connected to. Let's go straight to the point.

NestJS Documentation is great for the most part but it has some important omissions on the multiple databases section.
 

Let's get to it

Define DB connection options

Following NestJS docs we will find the connection options example credentials directly set on code.

...
@Module({ imports: [ TypeOrmModule.forRoot({ type: 'mysql', host: 'localhost', port: 3306, username: 'root', password: 'root', database: 'test', entities: [Customer], synchronize: true, }), ],
})
...

Instead of having credentials exposed on the code like that we should use an environment configuration file or a similar strategy.

For this example we will make use of the @nestjs/config package to get the database credentials from an environment file.

...
@Module({ imports: [ ConfigModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true, }), TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync({ imports: [ConfigModule], inject: [ConfigService], useFactory: async (configService: ConfigService) => { return { type: 'mssql', host: configService.get('MAIN_DB_HOST'), port: parseInt(configService.get('MAIN_DB_PORT')), database: configService.get('MAIN_DB_DATABASE'), username: configService.get('MAIN_DB_USERNAME'), password: configService.get('MAIN_DB_PASSWORD'), schema: configService.get('MAIN_DB_SCHEMA'), entities: [Customer], synchronize: false, }; }, }),
...

We don't need to define a connection name four our first connection. It will be named 'default' by default 😬. For any other defined connections we will need to provide a name.

NestJS docs tells us that the name property should be added inside the options object, at the same level as host, port, etc.

...
@Module({ imports: [ TypeOrmModule.forRoot({ ...defaultOptions, host: 'user_db_host', entities: [User], }), TypeOrmModule.forRoot({ ...defaultOptions, name: 'albumsConnection', host: 'album_db_host', entities: [Album], }), ],
})
...

Now, because we are getting our credentials from the .env file and using forRootAsync we need to add the connection name property outside of the useFactory function 👀. This is a really important detail that is not mentioned anywhere in the docs.

The final app.module.ts file should look like this.

Entities

We have created two entities for the sake of this excercise. Customer entity will use the main db connection and AccessLog entity will use the secondary db connection. For more information about TypeOrm Entity and Repository pattern check out NestJS TypeOrm Docs and TypeOrm Docs.
 

Use Main DB Connection

In order to have access to the Customer entity TypeOrm functionality we need to import TypeOrm into the Customer module.

And this is how we use it in the Customer service.

Use Secondary DB Connection

As we did for the main connection and the Customer entity we need to import TypeOrm into the AccessLog module, but this time we will also need to pass a connection name to the TypeOrmModule.forFeature([Entities, ...], connectionName) method.

And this is how we use it in the AccessLog service.

Conclusion

✅ We managed to setup a multiple db configuration with credentials values coming from an environment file. ✅ Defined what connection should each entity use. ✅ With the knowledge of how to add a secondary connection we should be able to add many more as needed.

✅ Learned about the little gotchas when initializing TypeOrm asynchronously.

1. 曾经想要一个无法开源的特定应用程序吗？
  2. 通过帮助构建下一代 Linux 应用程序来扩大生态系统
  3. 开发应用程序不仅能带来满足感，还能让您发挥创造力
  4. 赚取一些额外的现金
  5. 通过免费培训资源培养新技能并提升您的简历

现在是为 Linux 开发应用程序的最佳时机。

支持的语言：C++、JavaScript、Python、Rust 和 Vala

在这里查看更多信息

查看Flatpak 的文档以获取更多信息。

您还可以查看指南、人机界面指南以及教程。

有用的网址

  • 等离子 5
  • 开发者文档

查看 KDE 框架 5.70.0 的发布公告

您还可以查看教程和设计指南以获取更多信息以及框架 API 文档。

基本操作系统

开始使用文档或先查看elementary.io 。

遵循此处包含的建议可带来许多好处：

  • 用户将更快地学会使用您的应用程序，因为它共享他们已经熟悉的常见元素。
  • 用户将更快地完成任务，因为您将拥有一个简单明了的界面设计，不会令人困惑或困难。
  • 您的应用程序将显示为桌面原生，并与默认应用程序具有相同的优雅外观。
  • 您的应用程序将更容易记录，因为预期的行为不需要解释。
  • 由于上述原因，您必须提供的支持数量（包括提交的问题数量）将会减少。

在为基本操作系统编写应用程序时“不需要”经验，因为：

假设没有为基本操作系统编写应用程序的经验，但基本的编程知识是

查看他们的开发者文档并开始使用您的第一个应用程序。

电子

通过将 Chromium 和 Node.js 嵌入到其二进制文件中，Electron 允许您维护一个 JavaScript 代码库并创建可在 Windows、macOS 和 Linux 上运行的跨平台应用程序——无需原生开发经验。

阅读有关性能和安全性的最佳实践。 并通过下载Electron Fiddle获得带有代码片段的惊人示例。

加入他们的Discord 服务器，从其他 Electron 应用程序开发人员那里获得建议。

有用的网址

你会成为一名 Linux 应用程序开发人员吗？

快乐编码！



原文:
  1. Ever wanted a specific application that wasn't available open-source?
  2. Broaden the ecosystem by helping to build the next Linux app generation
  3. Developing applications not only gives a sense of fulfillment but lets you be creative
  4. Earn some extra cash
  5. Develop new skills through free training resources and level up your resume

The GNOME platform has new components, tools and docs. There's never been a better time to make apps for Linux.

Supported languages: C++, JavaScript, Python, Rust, and Vala

Builder is GNOME's IDE designed for creating applications with the GNOME platform that combines technologies such as GTK, Glib and more. Check more info here

Flatpak is the framework for building, distributing and running apps on Linux that allows developers to easily make apps in a stable development environment. View Flatpak's docs for more information.

There are also guidelines, human interface guidelines as well as tutorials you can view.

KDE Framework

KDE Framework 5 is a collection os 82 libraries that serves as the basic building block of KDE software. KDE Framework 5 (KF5) uses CMake

Helpful Links

  • Kirigami UI Framework
  • Plasma 5
  • Developer Documentation

View the release announcement for KDE frameworks 5.70.0

There are also tutorials as well as design guidelines you can view for more information as well as Framework API docs.

Elementary OS

Get started with the documentation or view elementary.io first.

Adhering to the suggestions contained here provides many benefits:

  • Users will learn to use your app faster, because it shares common elements that they are already familiar with.
  • Users will accomplish tasks more quickly, because you will have a straight-forward interface design that isn't confusing or difficult.
  • Your app will appear native to the desktop, and share the same elegant look as default applications.
  • Your app will be easier to document, because an expected behavior does not require explanation.
  • The amount of support you will have to provide will decrease—including the number of issues filed—for the reasons above.

No experience is "needed" in writing apps for elementary OS because:

no experience in writing apps for elementary OS is assumed, but basic programming knowledge is

View their developer docs and get started with your first app.

Electron

Electron developers to build cross-platform desktop apps with JavaScript, HTML, and CSS and that includes Linux!

By embedding Chromium and Node.js into its binary, Electron allows you to maintain one JavaScript codebase and create cross-platform apps that work on Windows, macOS, and Linux — no native development experience required.

Read about best practices in performance and security. And get amazing examples with code snippets you can run by downloading Electron Fiddle.

Join their Discord server to get advice from other Electron app developers.

Helpful Links

Will you become a Linux app developer?

Happy coding!

item.title: 社区指南最佳实践和示例 | Community Guideline Best Practices and Examples
item.description:
在运行在线社区时，无论是论坛、聊天室、游戏、共享经济应用程序，还是其他允许用户生成内容的平台，都必须制定社区准则来制定您的文化目标并采取行动反对您的用户或其内容是合理的。 社区准则是关于如何行事的道路规则。 您的社区指南将帮助您的社区塑造您的愿景，进而促进健康的话语以及用户的保留和增长。 在本文中，我们将讨论一些围绕社区准则的最佳实践，并查看当今公司如何利用它们的示例。

定义您的社区目的或价值观

首先，列出你的目的或价值观。 这些应该简短明了，几乎没有歧义。 无论你决定从你的目标还是你的价值观开始，都取决于你，这两者都有很好的例子。 表达你的目标和价值观是一个重要的指导北极星，它引导你的社区从一开始就取得成功。

暴力、骚扰和其他类似类型的行为会阻碍人们表达自己，并最终削弱全球公共对话的价值。” 鼓励，不要气馁

鼓励规则可以引导用户朝着正确的方向有效利用您的社区。 这些可以包括平台应该用于什么的描述符。 例如，在您的指南中，您可以鼓励您的用户在需要帮助时使用您的社区来分享问题、为他人回答问题、开始和参与讨论、分享有用的提示或谈论您的产品，等等。一些。 在定义允许和不允许的部分中列出规则列表是司空见惯的。

他们甚至鼓励用户在得到他人帮助时表示感谢。 在编写社区指南时，不要贬低或贬低他人，包括呼吁人们的道德、为暴力辩护、声称他们是罪犯、称他们为不人道或促进歧视，这一点非常重要。 您的社区指南可能是人们加入您的社区时首先看到的内容，因此您希望从一开始就树立一个好榜样。

将您的指南放在首位和居中

您注册了多少次并勾选了“我已阅读并同意条款和条件”和“我已阅读并同意社区准则”，而没有实际阅读其中任何一个？ 这可能是互联网最大的谎言之一，尤其是因为众所周知，公司会在他们的条款和条件中埋没东西，期望人们不要阅读它们。

例如，Apple 说无论您从他们的商店购买音乐，您仍然没有真正拥有它，只有聆听的权利（如果您深入了解，他们还说您不能使用任何 Apple 的在线商店来创建生物武器……让你想知道那里发生了什么）。 当您注册 Spotify 时，您可以让他们访问您手机上存储的所有数据。 一家芬兰 IT 安全公司 F-Secure 曾在他们的免费 Wi-Fi 热点中添加一个条款，上面写着“ 接收者同意在永恒期间将他们的第一个孩子分配给我们”，只是为了看看是否有人真的在阅读.

关键是，如果您可以在社区注册时以简洁的方式向他们展示您的社区指南，那么他们更有可能真正阅读它们。 约会应用程序通常对此非常提前。 一个例子是 Bumble，他在用户注册时就展示了他们的社区指南。 如果用户在注册过程中通过扩展链接获得简短而甜蜜的信息，他们可以了解您所获得内容的一般要点，而不是完全忽略它。

在审核内容时保持透明并保持一致性

如果您想让您的社区满意，您首先需要在您的社区指南中制定您的社区规则。 你的规则应该简单明了。 在你的社区里什么是不允许的？

对于不愿意遵守他们的规则的人，他们包括另一个部分，“如果这些准则不适合我怎么办？” 他们告诉他们的社区，简而言之，这些规则可能不适合您，但如果您不遵守它们，您将被禁止。

规则很有帮助，因为它们为您的版主制定了一个始终遵循的框架，为您的用户提供了连续性和透明度的感觉。 它将所有用户置于公平的竞争环境中。

透明度的另一个衡量标准是让人们了解您的容忍政策。 对屡犯者有处罚吗？ 如果用户违反社区准则会发生什么？ 您是只是删除他们的内容还是整体禁止他们？ 允许多少不良行为？

某些平台使用户能够在其应用程序内本地报告内容。 您可能需要考虑布置工作流程，以便您的用户可以帮助您使您的社区与您的指导方针保持一致。

其他最佳实践

  • 更新您的用户指南。 随着社区的发展，您可能需要更改指导方针的某些方面，以保持您的社区如您所愿并保持您的品牌声誉完好无损。
  • 留出空间来公开讨论您的社区准则。 无论是通过电子邮件，还是通过您的频道等。让人们参与进来，这样如果他们有任何问题，他们可以联系到负责人。 这也有助于提高透明度。
  • 如果您想建立一个强大的社区，请尽量避免“权力疯狂”，因为没有更好的术语。 如果您看到一个您不喜欢的对话，那么在采取严厉措施（例如禁止用户）之前，有很多机会尝试解决问题。 一些行动包括将对话转向其他方向（在公共论​​坛的情况下），或单独联系社区准则破坏者。
  • 如果它对您有意义，请不要害怕玩得开心。 上面链接的许多社区指南示例有助于从一开始就为他们的社区设定基调。

结论

当您创建强大的社区指南并以预先的方式向您的用户展示时，您就是在为您的社区未来发展奠定基础。 您的指导方针对于培养健康的社区很重要，因此请花时间鼓励您的社区使用上述最佳实践。

欲了解更多信息，请查看cleanspeak.com 。



原文:

When running an online community, whether it is a forum, chat room, game, sharing economy app, or other platform that allows user-generated content, it is imperative to set out community guidelines to lay out your cultural objectives and make taking action against your users or their content justifiable. Community guidelines are the rules of the road on how to behave. Your community guidelines will help mold your community to your vision, and in turn promote a healthy discourse as well as user retention and growth. In this article, we will discuss some of the best practices around community guidelines and look at examples of how companies are utilizing them today.

Define your community purpose or values

First, lay out your purpose or your values. These should be short and concise with little ambiguity. Whether you decide to start with your purpose or your values is up to you, and there are great examples of both. Expressing your purpose and values is an important guiding north star which leads your community to success from the beginning.

Twitter starts their community guidelines with a simple purpose statement before talking about their user limitations. “​​Twitter's purpose is to serve the public conversation. Violence, harassment and other similar types of behavior discourage people from expressing themselves, and ultimately diminish the value of global public conversation.” Twitter goes on to list their community rules and appeals process.

Buffer lists their community values as Positivity, Courage & Respect, and Thoughtful Language with explanations of what each value means to them, and what they should mean to the community.

Encourage, do not discourage

Rules of encouragement can lead users in the right direction to effectively utilize your community. These can include descriptors of what the platform should be utilized for. For example, in your guidelines you can encourage your users to use your community to share questions when they are in need of help, answer questions for others, start and join in on discussions, share helpful tips, or talk about your product, to name a few. It is commonplace to set out a list of rules in a section which defines what is allowed and what is not allowed.

Vodaphone UK does this by encouraging their community to be nice and share their knowledge for others if it should be useful. They even encourage users to say thanks when they’ve been helped by others. Vodaphone UK has a very succinct summary of their community rules within their guidelines where they offer a list of “please do’s” and “please don’ts.”

It is very important when writing your community guidelines to not disparage or belittle people, including calling out peoples’ morals, justifying violence, claiming them as criminals, referring to them as inhuman, or promoting discrimination. Your community guidelines may be the first thing people see when they join your community, so you want to set a good example from the beginning.

Get your guidelines front and center

How many times have you signed up for something and checked the boxes “I have read and agree to the terms and conditions” and “I have read and agree to the community guidelines,” without actually reading either of them? It may be one of the biggest lies of the internet, especially since companies have been known to bury things in their terms and conditions expecting people not to read them.

For example, Apple says no matter if you buy music off their store, you still do not actually own it, only the right to listen to it (if you dig in, they also say you cannot use any of Apple’s online stores to create biological weapons… makes you wonder what happened there). When you sign up for Spotify, you give them access to all the data stored on your phone. A Finnish IT security company, F-Secure, once added in a clause in their free Wi-Fi hotspot saying “the recipient agreed to assign their first born child to us for the duration of eternity,” just to see if anybody was actually reading.

The point is this, if you can present your community guidelines in a concise manner to your community as they sign up, they are more likely to actually read them. Dating apps usually are very upfront with this. One example is Bumble who presents their community guidelines right as users sign up. If users get the short and sweet during the signup process with a link to expand, they can understand the general gist of what you’re getting at, instead of ignoring it altogether.

Be transparent and have consistency in moderating content

If you want to keep your community happy, you will want to start by laying out your community rules inside of your community guidelines. Your rules should be straightforward. What is not allowed in your community?

Lyft explains their rules in the context of their values in a positive light. For people not willing to comply with their rules, they include another section, “What if these guidelines aren’t for me?” where they tell their community, in short, these rules may not be for you, but if you are not going to follow them, you will be banned.

Rules are helpful as they lay out a framework for your moderators to consistently follow which provides your users with a sense of continuity and transparency. It puts all users on a level playing field.

Another measure of transparency is letting people know about your tolerance policy. Is there a penalty for repeat offenders? What will happen if the user goes against the community guidelines? Are you just removing their content or banning them as a whole? How much bad behavior is allowed?

Some platforms give users the ability to report content natively inside of their app. Google Help adds in their participation agreement, “If you believe that someone is violating these posting guidelines, flag their post or thread for review: […] Report Abuse.” You may want to consider laying out the workflow so your users can help you align your community with your guidelines.

Other best practices

  • Keep your user guidelines updated. As your community evolves you may need to change certain facets of your guidelines to keep your community as you would like it and your brand reputation intact.
  • Allow a space for open discussion about your community guidelines. Whether by email, on your channels, etc. Keep people involved so that if they have any problems, they can reach out to whoever is in charge. This can also help boost transparency.
  • If you want to foster a strong community, try to refrain from being “power crazy,” for lack of a better term. If you see a conversation you do not like there are many opportunities to try to resolve the issue before taking drastic action such as banning a user. Some actions include steering conversations in other directions (in the case of public forums), or reaching out to community guideline breakers individually.
  • Do not be afraid to have fun with it if it makes sense for you. Many of the community guidelines examples linked above help set the tone they would like for their community right from the beginning.

Conclusion

When you create strong community guidelines and present them in an upfront manner to your users you are laying the foundational bricks for what your community will become. Your guidelines are important to fostering a healthy community, so take the time to encourage your community using the best practices described above.

For more info, check out cleanspeak.com.

item.author: jayzho

item.title: 区块链的五个基本原则 | Five Basic Principles of Blockchain
item.description:
有了区块链，我们可以想象一个合约嵌入数字代码并存储在透明共享数据库中的世界，在那里它们受到保护，不会被删除、篡改和修改。 在这个世界上，每一个协议、每一个流程、每一个任务和每一笔支付都将有一个可以识别、验证、存储和共享的数字记录和签名。

可能不再需要律师、经纪人和银行家等中介。 个人、组织、机器和算法可以自由地相互交易和互动，几乎没有摩擦。

这就是区块链的巨大潜力。

以下是该技术的五个基本原则：

区块链上的每一方都可以访问整个数据库及其完整历史。 没有任何一方控制数据或信息。 每一方都可以直接验证其交易伙伴的记录，无需中介。

通信直接发生在对等点之间，而不是通过中央节点。 每个节点存储信息并将信息转发给所有其他节点。

任何有权访问系统的人都可以看到每笔交易及其相关价值。 区块链上的每个节点或用户都有一个唯一的 30 多个字符的字母数字地址来标识它。 用户可以选择保持匿名或向他人提供身份证明。 交易发生在区块链地址之间。

一旦在数据库中输入交易并更新账户，记录就无法更改，因为它们与之前的每条交易记录相关联（因此称为“链”）。 部署了各种计算算法和方法，以确保数据库上的记录是永久的、按时间顺序排列的，并且可供网络上的所有其他人使用。

账本的数字性质意味着区块链交易可以与计算逻辑相关联，并且本质上是可编程的。 因此用户可以设置算法和规则来自动触发节点之间的交易。

回顾，HB 区块链。 哈佛商业评论出版社。 可在： https ://www.perlego.com/book/870419/blockchain-pdf （访问日期：2022 年 5 月 6 日）。



原文:

With blockchain, we can imagine a world in which contracts are embedded in digital code and stored in transparent, shared databases, where they are protected from deletion, tampering, and revision. In this world every agreement, every process, every task, and every payment would have a digital record and signature that could be identified, validated, stored, and shared.

Intermediaries like lawyers, brokers, and bankers might no longer be necessary. Individuals, organizations, machines, and algorithms would freely transact and interact with one another with little friction.

This is the immense potential of blockchain.

Here are five basic principles underlying the technology:

1- Distributed database. Each party on a blockchain has access to the entire database and its complete history. No single party controls the data or the information. Every party can verify the records of its transaction partners directly, without an intermediary.

2- Peer-to-peer transmission. Communication occurs directly between peers instead of through a central node. Each node stores and forwards information to all other nodes.

3- Transparency with pseudonymity. Every transaction and its associated value are visible to anyone with access to the system. Each node, or user, on a blockchain has a unique 30-plus-character alphanumeric address that identifies it. Users can choose to remain anonymous or provide proof of their identity to others. Transactions occur between blockchain addresses.

4- Irreversibility of records. Once a transaction is entered in the database and the accounts are updated, the records cannot be altered, because they’re linked to every transaction record that came before them (hence the term “chain”). Various computational algorithms and approaches are deployed to ensure that the recording on the database is permanent, chronologically ordered, and available to all others on the network.

5- Computational logic. The digital nature of the ledger means that blockchain transactions can be tied to computational logic and in essence programmed. So users can set up algorithms and rules that automatically trigger transactions between nodes.

--

Review, H. B. Blockchain. [edition unavailable]. Harvard Business Review Press. Available at: https://www.perlego.com/book/870419/blockchain-pdf (Accessed: 6 May 2022).

item.title: AWS Lambda 是否适合 ETL 作业？ | Does AWS Lambda good for ETL jobs?
item.description:
由于 AWS Lambda 是一个计算单元，使用成本低廉，并且可以实现或组合的功能多样，使其成为此类工作的一个有吸引力的选择。 但真的吗？

假设您有一个包含大量行和大量列的 Excel 工作表，您需要以特定的顺序或形状清理、转换和存储这些行。 你需要有某些组件来完成这个任务。

此外，您可以使用 EventBridge 创建一系列适合您配置的自定义架构的操作。 因此，我们可以确保 Lambda 有底层架构和连接性来完成这项工作。

当然不。 它有自己的一组功能和选项。 但在连接性方面，它无法与 Lambda 相提并论。 是的，Lambda 是一个计算单元，Glue 声明为数据集成服务。 但是，我们在这里所做的是比较用例本身，而不是限制。

可以在 Lambda 中完成的事情，可以在任何 EC2 系列中完成。 您只需要找到合适的家庭来完成这项工作。 但是，请记住，EC2 是一项客户托管服务，您必须小心处理。
这里有一个小评论，如果您的 ETL 消耗大量 CPU，请不要使用 T 系列。

还有一点我想澄清一下，利用 Lambda 可以利用的功能，如果代码出现意外数据类型（例如日期时间格式的差异）等问题，它可以恢复工作。 这种连接性在其他服务中没有清楚地呈现，这可能会让您在手动修复代码后重新运行整个文件。 相信我，我去过那里。

让我们从缺点开始：

时间：您不能正常运行作业超过 15 分钟。 拆分文件、运行许多功能并保持传递的数据完整性需要的不仅仅是这些。 权力：它非常有限，你可以分配什么，很难知道分配多少内存来分配 CPU。

错误配置：由于它是一个计算单元，您的代码可能是错误配置。 在代码中使用错误的函数可能会导致许多影响内存分配并造成瓶颈的问题。



原文:

ETL stands for: “Extract, Transform, Load”, which is the process of dealing with a series of data in a computing unit.
Since AWS Lambda is a computing unit, cheap to use, and versatile with what you can achieve or combine with, makes it an appealing option for this kind of jobs. But does it?

1- What ETL Jobs need to finish successfully?

Let’s assume that you have an excel sheet with a huge amount of rows along with a respectful number of columns, and you need to clean, transform and store these rows in a specific order or shape. You need to have certain components to achieve this task.

AWS Lambda provides these sets by being able to connect with storage services like S3, Glacier. Also, you can use EventBridge to create a series of actions that fits with the custom schemas you’ve configured. DynamoDB gives the ability to use NoSQL option that provides a pretty fast DB connectivity. SQS for queueing the data into the function, and catch the unprocessed data for later adjustment in the DLQ.

So, we can assure that the underlying architecture and connectivity is there for Lambda to do the job.

2- Only Lambda can do the job?

Of course NO. AWS has another service that called Glue, which is a Data Integration service that built to do these type of operation. It has its own set of features and options. But it cannot be compared to Lambda when it comes to the connectivity. Lambda has more options. Yes Lambda is a computing unit and Glue declared as Data Integration service. But, what we’re doing here is to compare the use case itself, not the limitations.

What can be done in Lambda, can be done in any EC2 family. You just need to find the right family to do the job. But, keep in mind that EC2 is a customer managed service, you have to take care of.
One small comment here, if your ETL is consuming a lot of CPU, do not use T family.

Another point I want to clarify that with what Lambda can utalize, it can recover the job if an issue comes to the code like unexpected data type like a difference in datetime format. Lambda can redirect these rows into DLQ which can work as a net that catch the uncommon data rows. This connectivity isn’t presented clearly in other services that might made you rerun the whole file after fixing the code manually. Trust me I’ve been there.

3- Advantages vs Disadvantages:

Let’s start with with the disadvantages:

Time: you cannot run the job normally for more than 15 minutes. It needs more than that to split the file, run many functions and maintain the passed data integrity. Power: it is very limited to what you can allocate, it is hard to know the right amount of memory to allocate which comes to CPU allocation.

Misconfiguration: since its a computing unit, your code might be the misconfiguration. Using the wrong function in your code could lead to many issues which affects the allocation of memory and create bottleneck.

item.title: 使用 Nokogiri 和 RegEx 在 Ruby 中扫描网页 | Using Nokogiri and RegEx to Scan a Webpage in Ruby
item.description:
我们将使用 gem 在网络文章中搜索主题以生成片段列表。

阅读我写的这篇文章以获得对 Nokogiri 的介绍。

我是棒球的普通粉丝。 他们在谈论什么？ 这是一个谜！ 我们需要用 Nokogiri 扫描页面来找出答案。 你准备好了吗？

获取那篇文章！

 ➜ ~ irb -- simple - prompt >> require 'nokogiri' => true >> require 'open-uri' => true # I'm going to scan the article at this URL. Feel free to # find one of your own, since this will probably be outdated # by the time you read this. >> url = 'https://www.espn.com/mlb/story/_/id/33855507/mlb-power-rankings-week-4 -new-team-surged-our-no-1-spot' => "https://www.espn.com/mlb/story/_/id/33855507/mlb-power-rankings-week-4-... >> web_page = Nokogiri::HTML5(URI(url)) => #(Document:0x5b4 { ...

现在我们需要找到这篇文章的正文，所以我们只扫描相关部分。 我们将使用带有 div 和类名的#css方法。

 >> body = web_page . css ( 'div.article-body' ) => [ #<Nokogiri::XML::Element:0x5730 name="div" attributes=[#<Nokogiri::XML:... >> text = body . text => "7:00 AM ETESPNFacebookTwitterFacebook MessengerPinterestEmailprintFour ...

现在是有趣的部分。

正则表达式扫描

但我们真的不想只得到一个事件列表。 我们想要获得整个上下文，以便我们可以看到文章在说什么。 为此，我们需要找到每个出现的索引，就好像我们的字符串是一个字符数组一样。 然后我们将在该索引之前和之后进行切片以获取每次出现的上下文。 这带来了一个（可能鲜为人知的）方法，称为#to_enum （到枚举器）。

这是一个示例，我们将获取字符串中每个 ASCII 字符的字节码。 我们将使用to_s(2)将每个打印到二进制文件。

 >> "abcdefg" . to_enum ( :each_byte ). each { | b | p b . to_s ( 2 ) } "1100001" "1100010" "1100011" "1100100" "1100101" "1100110" "1100111"

出于我们的目的，我们将传递:scan方法，其参数是我们的正则表达式。 然后我们将使用Regexp.last_match.begin(0)映射每个事件，以获取该事件的开始索引。 这就是它的工作原理。

 # remember text holds the text of the article body # each index will go into the indices variable # we can search for whatever we want, let's search for pitch # this will work for pitch, pitchers, pitches, etc. >> indices = text . to_enum ( :scan , /pitch/i ). map do | pitch | Regexp . last_match . begin ( 0 ) >> end => [ 1825 , ... >> indices => [ 1825 , 3699 , 4727 , 10007 , 10127 , 10846 , 11016 , 12617 , 13734 , 14060 , 14585 , 14927 , 16019 , 17835 , 18858 ]

伟大的！ 这个索引列表向我们揭示了从哪里切片来获取我们的数据。 我们将在开始前切片 30 个字符，并将切片的长度设置为 70 个字符。 我们会将这些文本片段推送到一个数组中。

 >> snippets = [] => [] ?> indices . each do | i | ?> snippets << text . slice ( i - 30 , 70 ) >> end >> snippets => [ "n-differential in the majors. Pitching has mostly carried them, but th" , "st year, Milwaukee's starting pitching was basically three deep. That " , "rt envious: Too many starting pitchers. Clevinger is the sixth member " , " allowed. While he has a five-pitch repertoire, one thing he's done th" , "eup combo. He threw those two pitches a combined 64.3% of the time las" , "ause his swing rate and first-pitch swing rate in particular are up. H" , "nd him he's going to get more pitches to hit. -- Schoenfield17. Chicag" , "2 batting line this year. The pitching staff has been one of the brigh" , "ice start. Good, right-handed pitching will stymie them this year, tho" , "le against both hard and soft pitching, despite dominating the league " , " ranks among some of the best pitchers in all of baseball in WAR. -- L" , " back to .500. Their starting pitchers have lifted them, with Zac Gall" , ". The Rangers did have better pitching last week, moving them up the l" , "r nine innings in 11⅓ innings pitched. -- Lee29. Washington NationalsR" , " Colorado will do that -- but pitching was a big problem. The Reds com" ]

我们做到了！ 现在让我们清理它们，使它们以完整的单词开头和结尾。 我们将获取每个片段，用空格将其分开，删除第一个和最后一个部分单词，然后将其与空格一起粘贴回去。

 snippets . map do | snippet | ?> words = snippet . split ( " " ) ?> words . pop ?> words . shift ?> snippet = words . join ( " " ) >> end => [ "in the majors. Pitching has mostly carried them, but" , "year, Milwaukee's starting pitching was basically three deep." , "envious: Too many starting pitchers. Clevinger is the sixth" , "While he has a five-pitch repertoire, one thing he's done" , "combo. He threw those two pitches a combined 64.3% of the time" , "his swing rate and first-pitch swing rate in particular are up." , "him he's going to get more pitches to hit. -- Schoenfield17." , "batting line this year. The pitching staff has been one of the" , "start. Good, right-handed pitching will stymie them this year," , "against both hard and soft pitching, despite dominating the" , "among some of the best pitchers in all of baseball in WAR. --" , "to .500. Their starting pitchers have lifted them, with Zac" , "The Rangers did have better pitching last week, moving them up the" , "nine innings in 11⅓ innings pitched. -- Lee29. Washington" , "will do that -- but pitching was a big problem. The Reds" ]

你有它！ 希望本文仅揭示了 Ruby 的一些很酷的特性。 不要害怕探索新的宝石并在 Ruby-Doc 网站上查找新方法。

特蕾西-格雷戈里·吉尔摩—— Feb 28 2月28日



原文:

We are going to use a gem to search for a topic in a web article to produce a list of snippets.

Read this article I wrote to get an introduction to Nokogiri.

I'm a casual fan of into baseball. ESPN has lots of MLB articles. What do they talk about? It's a mystery! We need to scan the page with Nokogiri to find out. Are you ready?

Get that Article! 

➜ ~ irb --simple-prompt
>> require 'nokogiri'
=> true
>> require 'open-uri'
=> true # I'm going to scan the article at this URL. Feel free to 
# find one of your own, since this will probably be outdated 
# by the time you read this. >> url = 'https://www.espn.com/mlb/story/_/id/33855507/mlb-power-rankings-week-4
-new-team-surged-our-no-1-spot'
=> "https://www.espn.com/mlb/story/_/id/33855507/mlb-power-rankings-week-4-...
>> web_page = Nokogiri::HTML5(URI(url))
=>
#(Document:0x5b4 {
...

Now we need to find the body of this article so we only scan the relevant portions. We'll use the #css method with the div and class name.

>> body = web_page.css('div.article-body')
=> [#<Nokogiri::XML::Element:0x5730 name="div" attributes=[#<Nokogiri::XML:...
>> text = body.text
=> "7:00 AM ETESPNFacebookTwitterFacebook MessengerPinterestEmailprintFour ...

Now for the fun part.

Regular Expression Scan

Ruby has a String#scan method that takes a Regular Expression as an argument and returns an array of all occurrences.

>> "don't do that dog!".scan(/do/) => ["do", "do", "do"]

But we don't really want to just get a list of occurrences. We want to get the whole context so that we can see what the article is talking about. To accomplish this, we need to find the index of each occurrence as if our string was an array of characters. Then we will slice well before and after this index to get the context of each occurrence. This brings up a (maybe lesser-known) method called #to_enum (to enumerator).

The to_enum method allows us to enumerate the String and pass a method and optional argument. Here's an example where we will get the byte code for each ASCII character in a string. We will print each to binary using to_s(2).

>> "abcdefg".to_enum(:each_byte).each { |b| p b.to_s(2) }
"1100001"
"1100010"
"1100011"
"1100100"
"1100101"
"1100110"
"1100111"

For our purposes, we will pass the :scan method with the argument being our Regular Expression. Then we will map each occurrence with Regexp.last_match.begin(0) to get the beginning index for the occurrence. This is how it works.

# remember text holds the text of the article body
# each index will go into the indices variable
# we can search for whatever we want, let's search for pitch
# this will work for pitch, pitchers, pitches, etc.
>> indices = text.to_enum(:scan, /pitch/i).map do |pitch| Regexp.last_match.begin(0)
>> end
=>
[1825,
...
>> indices
=>
[1825, 3699, 4727, 10007, 10127, 10846, 11016, 12617, 13734, 14060, 14585, 14927, 16019, 17835, 18858]

Great! This list of indices reveals to us where to slice to get our data. We'll slice 30 characters before the start and will will make the length of our slice 70 characters. We'll push these snippets of text into an array.

>> snippets = []
=> []
?> indices.each do |i|
?> snippets << text.slice(i - 30, 70)
>> end >> snippets
=>
["n-differential in the majors. Pitching has mostly carried them, but th", "st year, Milwaukee's starting pitching was basically three deep. That ", "rt envious: Too many starting pitchers. Clevinger is the sixth member ", " allowed. While he has a five-pitch repertoire, one thing he's done th", "eup combo. He threw those two pitches a combined 64.3% of the time las", "ause his swing rate and first-pitch swing rate in particular are up. H", "nd him he's going to get more pitches to hit. -- Schoenfield17. Chicag", "2 batting line this year. The pitching staff has been one of the brigh", "ice start. Good, right-handed pitching will stymie them this year, tho", "le against both hard and soft pitching, despite dominating the league ", " ranks among some of the best pitchers in all of baseball in WAR. -- L", " back to .500. Their starting pitchers have lifted them, with Zac Gall", ". The Rangers did have better pitching last week, moving them up the l", "r nine innings in 11⅓ innings pitched. -- Lee29. Washington NationalsR", " Colorado will do that -- but pitching was a big problem. The Reds com"]

We did it! Now let's clean them up so they start and end with full words. We'll take each snippet, split it apart by whitespace, remove the first and last partial words, then paste it back together with spaces.

snippets.map do |snippet|
?> words = snippet.split(" ")
?> words.pop
?> words.shift
?> snippet = words.join(" ")
>> end
=>
["in the majors. Pitching has mostly carried them, but", "year, Milwaukee's starting pitching was basically three deep.", "envious: Too many starting pitchers. Clevinger is the sixth", "While he has a five-pitch repertoire, one thing he's done", "combo. He threw those two pitches a combined 64.3% of the time", "his swing rate and first-pitch swing rate in particular are up.", "him he's going to get more pitches to hit. -- Schoenfield17.", "batting line this year. The pitching staff has been one of the", "start. Good, right-handed pitching will stymie them this year,", "against both hard and soft pitching, despite dominating the", "among some of the best pitchers in all of baseball in WAR. --", "to .500. Their starting pitchers have lifted them, with Zac", "The Rangers did have better pitching last week, moving them up the", "nine innings in 11⅓ innings pitched. -- Lee29. Washington", "will do that -- but pitching was a big problem. The Reds"]

There you have it! Hopefully this article revealed just some of the cool features to Ruby. Don't be afraid to explore new gems and look up new methods on the Ruby-Doc website.

Tracy-Gregory Gilmore - Feb 28

item.description:
彼得·梅哈伊尔

发表于May 7 5月7日

Svelte nodes have a __svelte_meta object in development mode that contains the file location of the component that rendered that node. Svelte 节点在开发模式下有一个__svelte_meta对象，其中包含呈现该节点的组件的文件位置。

 { "loc" : { "file" : "src/routes/index.svelte" , "line" : 18 , "column" : 4 , "char" : 358 }
}

您可以在StackBlitz上尝试一下。 使用 Chrome 开发工具检查元素并使用控制台：

了解有关__svelte_meta更多信息：

扎基·阿卜杜拉赫曼—— Apr 18 4月18日



原文:

Peter Mekhaeil

Posted on May 7

Svelte nodes have a __svelte_meta object in development mode that contains the file location of the component that rendered that node.

{ "loc": { "file": "src/routes/index.svelte", "line": 18, "column": 4, "char": 358 }
}

You an try it out on StackBlitz. Inspect an element using the Chrome Dev Tools and use the console:

($0 references the last selected DOM element. It is part of the DevTool's Console API)

item.author: petermekhaeil

item.title: 第 28 天：无休止的工作 | Day 28: Endless work
item.description:
伊瓦德·伊夫·哈比马纳

发表于你好！ 现在是 03:15，现在是星期五。 通常在星期五我早睡，因为没有比赛可做，周末我有空闲时间，但这次不同，我仍在处理我的个人投资组合项目，我取得了巨大的进步，我在 Trello 上创建了一个项目管理委员会有卡片和一些我设法完成了我和我的队友进行了一次很棒的编码会议，我也不觉得很累

明天（技术上今天）我计划至少完成复杂部分的论文，我也必须为这个项目腾出时间，因为它必须尽快完成我还计划写一篇关于setting up testing environment with Jest and React testing Library的文章setting up testing environment with Jest and React testing Library

我们将看看它会如何发展顺便说一句，距离第一个里程碑还有 2 天！

签字...

蒂亚戈·马萨里·格德斯 - Mar 16 3月16日



原文:

Ivad Yves HABIMANA

Posted on May 7

Hi! It's now 03:15 and it's Friday. usually on Fridays I sleep early because there no match to do and I have free time in the weekends but this one was different, I was still working on my personal portfolio project and I made a huge progress where I created a project management board on Trello with cards and some I managed to finish them I had a great coding session with on of my teammates and I don't feel that tired either

Tomorrow ( technically today) I plan to finish the paper at least the complex parts and I have to spare time for the project too because it have to be done as soon as possible I also plan to write an article about setting up testing environment with Jest and React testing Library

We will see how it will come along We are 2 days away from the first milestone by the way!

signing off...

Thiago Massari Guedes - Mar 16
item.author: yvad60

item.title: 开源冒险：第 54 集：BATTLETECH 武器排名应用程序 | Open Source Adventures: Episode 54: BATTLETECH Weapon Ranking App
item.description:
托马斯·韦格扎诺夫斯基

发表于让我们开始编写应用程序。 现在我将硬编码一些静态假设，例如：

  • 我们不在乎暴击伤害
  • 我们不在乎稳定性损坏
  • 每个武器 10 弹药
  • 我每次都没有耐心自己写，所以我们将使用lodash作为它的sortBy函数。 它的 API 很不方便，但它可以工作。

    App.svelte

     <script> import data from " ./data.json " import Row from " ./Row.svelte " import { sortBy } from " lodash " let round100 = ( v ) => Math . round ( v * 100 ) / 100 for ( let row of data ) { row . value = row . shots * row . baseDamage row . ammoWeight = round100 ( 10 * row . ammoTonnagePerShot ) row . cost = round100 ( row . tonnage + row . ammoWeight + row . heat / 3.0 ) row . ratio = round100 ( row . value / row . cost ) } let sortedData = sortBy ( data , [( x ) => - x . ratio , ( x ) => x . name ]) </script> <h1> BATTLETECH Weapons Data </h1> <table> <tr> <th> Name </th> <th> Damage </th> <th> Heat </th> <th> Weight </th> <th> Ammo Weight </th> <th> Range </th> <th> Value </th> <th> Cost </th> <th> Ratio </th> </tr> {#each sortedData as row} <Row data= {row} /> {/each} </table> <style> :global ( body ) { margin : 0 ; min-height : 100vh ; display : flex ; flex-direction : column ; justify-content : center ; align-items : center ; } table :global ( tr ) :nth-child ( even ) { background-color : #f2f2f2 ; } table :global ( tr ) :nth-child ( odd ) { background-color : #e0e0e0 ; } </style>

    这是主要的组成部分。 我们在这里静态地预先计算各种派生数据。 这些假设将变成滑块。

    其他几点：

    • 这不是唯一的方法，但它会做
    • 这个组件要容易得多。 伤害和范围列需要一些自定义格式。 使用一些彩色条代替范围列肯定会好得多。

      四舍五入到小数点后 2 位可以说是在这里而不是在App组件中处理。

       <script> export let data let { name , heat , shots , baseDamage , tonnage , minRange , maxRange , value , cost , ratio , ammoWeight } = data let damage if ( shots == 1 ) { damage = baseDamage } else { damage = ` ${ shots } x ${ baseDamage } ` } let range = ` ${ minRange } - ${ maxRange } ` </script> <tr> <td> {name} </td> <td> {damage} </td> <td> {heat} </td> <td> {tonnage} </td> <td> {ammoWeight} </td> <td> {range} </td> <td> {value} </td> <td> {cost} </td> <td> {ratio} </td> </tr>

      到目前为止的故事

      所有代码都在 GitHub 上。

      我在 GitHub Pages 上部署了它，你可以在这里看到它。

      接下来

      在下一集中，我将添加一些滑块来控制所做的假设。



原文:

Tomasz Wegrzanowski

Posted on May 7

Let's start coding the app. For now I'll hardcode some static assumptions like:

  • we don't care about crit damage
  • we don't care about stability damage
  • 10 ammo per weapon
  • 100% of heat compensated by single heat sinks, so 1 extra ton per 3 heat

Lodash sortBy

JavaScript standard library has an embarassing lack of a lot of basic functionality. I have little patience for writing it all myself every time, so we'll be using lodash for its sortBy function. Its API is quite inconvenient, but it works.

App.svelte 

<script> import data from "./data.json" import Row from "./Row.svelte" import {sortBy} from "lodash" let round100 = (v) => Math.round(v * 100) / 100 for (let row of data) { row.value = row.shots * row.baseDamage row.ammoWeight = round100(10 * row.ammoTonnagePerShot) row.cost = round100(row.tonnage + row.ammoWeight + row.heat/3.0) row.ratio = round100(row.value / row.cost) } let sortedData = sortBy(data, [(x) => -x.ratio, (x) => x.name])
</script> <h1>BATTLETECH Weapons Data</h1> <table> <tr> <th>Name</th> <th>Damage</th> <th>Heat</th> <th>Weight</th> <th>Ammo Weight</th> <th>Range</th> <th>Value</th> <th>Cost</th> <th>Ratio</th> </tr> {#each sortedData as row} <Row data={row} /> {/each}
</table> <style>
:global(body) { margin: 0; min-height: 100vh; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center;
}
table :global(tr):nth-child(even) { background-color: #f2f2f2;
}
table :global(tr):nth-child(odd) { background-color: #e0e0e0;
}
</style>

Here's the main component. We statically precalculate various derived data here. These assumptions will turn into sliders.

Some other points:

  • there's fairly messy :global to make odd/even shading work with tables across component boundary; it's not the only way, but it will do
  • let sortedData = sortBy(data, [(x) => -x.ratio, (x) => x.name]) is some really awkward API

Row.svelte

This component is much easier. Damage and Range column need some custom formatting. Range column could sure be a lot better with some colored bars instead.

Rounding to 2 decimal places could arguably be handled here instead of in the App component.

<script> export let data let {name, heat, shots, baseDamage, tonnage, minRange, maxRange, value, cost, ratio, ammoWeight} = data let damage if (shots == 1) { damage = baseDamage } else { damage = `${shots}x${baseDamage}` } let range = `${minRange}-${maxRange}`
</script> <tr> <td>{name}</td> <td>{damage}</td> <td>{heat}</td> <td>{tonnage}</td> <td>{ammoWeight}</td> <td>{range}</td> <td>{value}</td> <td>{cost}</td> <td>{ratio}</td>
</tr>

Story so far

All the code is on GitHub.

I deployed this on GitHub Pages, you can see it here.

Coming next

In the next episode I'll add some sliders to control assumptions made.
item.author: taw

item.title: 如何提高网站速度：采取七个可行的步骤 | How to increase the speed of your website: seven actionable steps to take
item.description:
当涉及到您网站的性能时，页面速度至关重要。

然而，除非您了解该指标的工作原理，更具体地说，了解影响它的因素，否则您可能会发现很难改进。

让您的网页快速、流畅地加载以降低跳出率并增加访问者参与度至关重要。

一个针对速度进行了优化的网站不仅可以改善用户体验 (UX)，还可以帮助您在搜索引擎中排名更高。

在本文中，我将解释为什么它很重要，并向您展示如何衡量它。 那我就给大家七种提高页面加载速度的解决方案。

为什么页面速度很重要？
如前所述，网站速度可以快速确定用户是否留在您的网站上。 但它甚至比这更重要。 在确定哪些网站显示在搜索引擎结果页面的顶部时，搜索引擎，尤其是 Google，在确定哪些网站显示在搜索引擎结果页面 (SERP) 的顶部时，会非常重视现场速度。 网站速度优化提高了搜索引擎优化 (SEO)，因此不容忽视。

为了测量页面速度，谷歌的 PageSpeed Insights非常适合。 如何提高页面速度

优化图像
大多数网页由于图像文件大而速度慢，这会导致页面加载时间的巨大增加。 为页面设置资产时。 通过减小文件大小或替换文件格式来优化图像。 使用tinyjpg.com优化您的图像。

如果您想要更小的尺寸，请使用 jpg 或 webp（推荐）文件格式。

缩小 Html、CSS 和 js
通过优化代码，您可以提高页面速度。 同时删除代码注释、格式和未使用的代码。 使用浏览器缓存
浏览器缓存是提高页面速度的最流行的方法之一。 通过在访问者的浏览器中缓存大量信息。 浏览器在返回时不必重新加载整个页面。 在此处了解有关利用浏览器缓存的更多信息

使用内容交付网络 (CDN)
内容交付网络 (CDN)，也称为内容分发网络，是分配交付内容负载的服务器网络。 本质上，您的站点副本存储在多个地理上不同的数据中心，以便用户可以更快、更可靠地访问您的站点。 在此处了解如何在您的网站上使用 CDN

减少重定向
当一个页面重定向到另一个页面时，您的访问者必须等待 HTTP 请求-响应周期完成。 例如，如果您的移动重定向模式如下：

每个重定向都会减慢页面的加载时间。

移除阻止渲染的 JavaScript
在呈现页面之前，浏览器必须首先通过解析 HTML 来构建 DOM 树。 如果您的浏览器在此过程中遇到脚本，它必须在继续之前停止并执行它。

对初始渲染不重要的脚本应该异步或延迟到第一次渲染之后。 可以内联渲染页面内容所需的脚本，以避免额外的网络请求； 但是，内联内容必须小而快才能提供良好的性能。 在此处了解有关如何删除阻止渲染的 JavaScript 的更多信息

提高服务器响应时间
您收到的流量、每个页面使用的资源、服务器上的软件以及您使用的托管解决方案都会影响您的服务器响应时间。 寻找性能瓶颈，例如数据库查询速度慢、路由速度慢或内存不足，并修复它们以提高服务器响应时间。 理想的服务器响应时间小于 200ms。 在此处了解有关如何改善服务器响应时间的更多信息

当您完美地选中所有这些步骤时，您的网站将处于最高速度。



原文:

When it comes to the performance of your website, page speed is crucial.

Yet, you may find it difficult to improve unless you understand how this metric works — and more specifically, the factors that influence it.

It is critical that your web pages load fast and smoothly to reduce your bounce rate and increase visitor engagement.

A site that is optimized for speed not only improves the user experience (UX) but can also help you rank higher in search engines.

In this article, I’ll explain why it's important, and show you how to measure it. Then I'll give you seven solutions for improving page loading speed.

Why is page speed important?
As before stated, site speed can quickly determine whether a user stays on your website. But it's even more significant than that. When determining which websites to show at the top of search engine results pages, search engines, particularly Google, place a high-value on-site speed when determining which websites show at the top of the search engine results page(SERP). Website speed optimization improves search engine optimization (SEO), so it shouldn’t be overlooked.

For measuring page speed, Google's PageSpeed Insights is fit for that. Google's PageSpeed Insights is a website speed testing tool that ranks your site's speed on a scale of 0 to 100, with higher scores being better.

Google PageSpeed Insights will generate tests for both desktop and mobile, as well as recommendations for how to improve your speed score.

How to increase your page speed

Optimize images
Most web pages are slow due to large image files which causes an enormous increase in load time for pages. When setting up your assets for a page. Optimize your images by reducing the file size or by replacing the file format. Optimize your images using tinyjpg.com.

Use jpg or webp(recommended) file format if you’re going for smaller sizes.

Minify Html, CSS, and js
By optimizing your code, you can increase your page speed. Also remove code comments, formatting, and unused code. Google recommends using HtmlMinifier, CSSNano, and UglifyJS.

Use browser caching
Browser caching is one of the most popular methods for increasing page speed. By caching a large amount of information in your visitors' browsers. The browser does not have to reload the entire page when they return. Learn more about leveraging browser caching here

Use a content delivery network(CDN)
Content delivery networks (CDNs), also known as content distribution networks are server networks that distribute the load of delivering content. Essentially, copies of your site are stored at multiple, geographically diverse data centers so that users can access your site more quickly and reliably. Learn how to use CDN on your site here

Reduce redirects
When a page redirects to another page, your visitor must wait for the HTTP request-response cycle to complete. For instance, if your mobile redirect pattern is as follows:

example.com -> www.example.com -> m.example.com -> m.example.com/home

Each redirect slows down the load time of your page.

Remove render-blocking JavaScript
Before they can render a page, browsers must first construct a DOM tree by parsing HTML. If your browser comes across a script during this process, it must stop and execute it before proceeding.

Scripts that are not critical to the initial render should be made asynchronous or deferred until after the first render. Scripts required to render page content can be inlined to avoid extra network requests; but, inlined content must be small and fast to deliver good performance. Learn more on how to Remove render-blocking JavaScript here

Improve server response time
The amount of traffic you receive, the resources used by each page, the software on your server, and the hosting solution you use all have an impact on your server response time. Look for performance bottlenecks such as slow database queries, slow routing, or a lack of enough memory, and fix them to improve your server response time. The ideal server response time is less than 200ms. Learn more on how to improve server response time here

When you checkmark all these step perfectly, Your site will be at its top speed.
item.author: devfarouqk

item.title: 我如何扫描 dev.to API 的漏洞 | How I scanned dev.to APIs for vulnerabilities
item.description:
我最近看到 dev.to 发布了关于他们的 REST API 的更新。
我开始好奇，我想扫描 Dev.to REST API 的基本漏洞。

我为这项工作使用了一个免费的基于 Web 的 API 安全工具。
以下是扫描结果

令人惊讶的是，它报告了 8 个问题。 这是列表：

我分析了 dev.to Web UI 以了解发生了什么。 我很快发现所有开放端点也在 Web UI 上打开，并且在设计上保持公开，因此未经身份验证的用户可以查看 dev.to 文章、视频及其相关标签、类别和作者的公共图像。 所有其他功能，如喜欢、评论、关注、创建文章等内容参与，都需要对用户进行身份验证。

免费的网络工具在识别未经身份验证的端点方面做得不错。 当然，该工具不可能猜到将这些端点公开的商业原因。

这是免费工具网址： https ://apisec-inc.github.io/pentest/



原文:

I recently saw the dev.to published an update on their REST APIs.
https://developers.forem.com/api

I became curious, and I wanted to scan the Dev.to REST API for basic vulnerabilities.

I used a free and web-based API security tool for this job.
https://apisec-inc.github.io/pentest/

Here are the scan results

Surprisingly it reported 8 issues. Here is the list:

I analyzed the dev.to web UI to find out what was happening. I quickly figured out all the open endpoints were also open on the web UI and were left public by design so the unauthenticated users can view the dev.to articles, videos, and their associated tags, categories, and author's public images. All other functionality like content engagement like likes, comments, follow, create articles, etc., requires the user to be authenticated.

The free web tool did a decent job of identifying unauthenticated endpoints. Of course, there was no way the tool could have guessed the business reasoning behind leaving those endpoints public.

Here is the free tool URL: https://apisec-inc.github.io/pentest/
item.author: intesar
